{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_69e5cdb86d3048c9bb20e0ac","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# aide-de-camp\n2\t\n3\t**A personal command interface.** Speak or type a stream-of-consciousness request — spanning multiple projects, questions, and tasks — and receive parallel, organized results on a live browser canvas or in audio.\n4\t\n5\tCLI alias: `adc`\n6\t\n7\t---\n8\t\n9\t## How It Works\n10\t\n11\t1. **Intent routing** — a single fast LLM call segments your utterance into typed intent threads, each tagged with a project, an intent type (`status`, `action`, `brainstorm`, `lookup`, `reminder`, `self-modification`, `monitoring-config`, `task-profile`, `clarification`), and an urgency level (`critical`, `high`, `normal`, `low`).\n12\t\n13\t2. **Parallel dispatch** — all threads run concurrently. A compound utterance touching five projects takes as long as the slowest single agent, not their sum.\n14\t\n15\t3. **Fetch strand** — each agent runs deterministic commands against live data sources (kubectl, git, local repos, SSH targets) to gather raw context for its thread.\n16\t\n17\t4. **Synthesize strand** — one LLM call per thread converts the raw data into a 2–3 sentence structured result.\n18\t\n19\t5. **Canvas delivery** — results stream to the browser via SSE and render as HTML cards, one per topic, updated in place as agents resolve.\n20\t\n21\t```\n22\t  Voice / Text Input\n23\t  \"has the pipeline caught up? also what's the ibkr mcp status?\"\n24\t                        │\n25\t                        ▼\n26\t             ┌──────────────────┐\n27\t             │   Intent Router  │  ← one LLM call, splits utterance\n28\t             └──────────────────┘\n29\t                        │\n30\t          ┌─────────────┴──────────────┐\n31\t          ▼                            ▼\n32\t   [agent: pipeline]           [agent: ibkr-mcp]\n33\t   Fetch → Synthesize           Fetch → Synthesize\n34\t          │                            │\n35\t          └──────────────┬─────────────┘\n36\t                         ▼\n37\t              ┌─────────────────────────┐\n38\t              │  Canvas (SSE)           │\n39\t              │  ┌─────────┐ ┌───────┐  │\n40\t              │  │pipeline │ │ibkr   │  │\n41\t              │  │ card    │ │ card  │  │\n42\t              │  └─────────┘ └───────┘  │\n43\t              └─────────────────────────┘\n44\t```\n45\t\n46\t**Task-profile intents** skip fetch/synthesize. Instead, a work item is created and picked up asynchronously by an async worker fleet. When the work completes, the result is pushed to the canvas via the bead watcher daemon.\n47\t\n48\t---\n49\t\n50\t## Features\n51\t\n52\t### Input methods\n53\t- **Text**: browser canvas at `http://localhost:8000/` or CLI: `adc dispatch \"...\"`\n54\t- **Voice**: OpenAI Realtime API (WebRTC) — speak directly into the browser\n55\t\n56\t### Live canvas\n57\tSingle-page dark-themed UI (`src/canvas/index.html`). Results stream in via Server-Sent Events. Each distinct topic (project + intent) gets its own card, updated in place as results arrive. No page reload needed.\n58\t\n59\t### Voice narration\n60\tResults are narrated at urgency-tiered conversational pauses:\n61\t- `critical` — interrupt immediately\n62\t- `high` — next natural pause\n63\t- `normal` — batched delivery\n64\t- `low` — only when idle\n65\t\n66\t### Self-modification and hot-reload\n67\tAll behavior-defining artifacts are plain files, re-read on every invocation:\n68\t- `prompts/router.md`, `prompts/synthesize.md`, `prompts/voice.md`, `prompts/urgency.md`\n69\t- `config/registry.yaml` — project and alias definitions\n70\t- `config/monitoring.yaml` — ambient monitoring rules\n71\t- `data/components.db` — versioned HTML/CSS card templates (SQLite)\n72\t\n73\tA spoken instruction like \"the deploy card is too cluttered\" triggers the `SelfModificationAgent`, which generates a diff, surfaces it for approval, and hot-reloads the artifact. No redeploy needed.\n74\t\n75\t### Project registry\n76\t`config/registry.yaml` defines explicit project entries with cluster targets, namespaces, aliases, and workflow templates. aide-de-camp also auto-discovers git repos at startup. Refresh via `POST /api/v1/environment/refresh`.\n77\t\n78\t### Session persistence\n79\tSession state lives in a SQLite WAL database (`data/session.db`). Tables: `sessions`, `surfaces`, `utterances`, `intents`, `topics`, `results`. The same session is accessible from the canvas, CLI, or audio mode simultaneously.\n80\t\n81\t---\n82\t\n83\t## Quick Start\n84\t\n85\t```bash\n86\t# Clone and install\n87\tgit clone <repo-url> aide-de-camp\n88\tcd aide-de-camp\n89\t\n90\tpip install -e \".[dev]\"\n91\t# or\n92\tpip install -r requirements.txt\n93\t\n94\t# Start the server\n95\tuvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000\n96\t\n97\t# Verify it's running\n98\tcurl -s http://localhost:8000/health\n99\t```\n100\t\n101\tOpen `http://localhost:8000/` in your browser to use the canvas.\n102\t\n103\tFor voice mode, an OpenAI API key with Realtime API access is required (set `OPENAI_API_KEY`).\n104\t\n105\t---\n106\t\n107\t## CLI Usage\n108\t\n109\t```bash\n110\t# Route an utterance and stream results to the canvas\n111\tadc dispatch \"has the options pipeline caught up?\"\n112\t\n113\t# Query a specific topic\n114\tadc ask \"what name candidates did we have for pdftract?\"\n115\t\n116\t# Current session summary\n117\tadc status\n118\t\n119\t# List active topic cards\n120\tadc topics\n121\t\n122\t# Show exception queue\n123\tadc exceptions\n124\t\n125\t# Configure the CLI\n126\tadc config --set-server http://myhost:8000\n127\tadc config --set-session <session-id>\n128\t\n129\t# Help\n130\tadc --help\n131\t```\n132\t\n133\t---\n134\t\n135\t## Configuration\n136\t\n137\t### Environment variables\n138\t\n139\t| Variable | Purpose | Default |\n140\t|----------|---------|---------|\n141\t| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | OpenAI key — required for voice/Realtime API | _(none; voice disabled without it)_ |\n142\t| `ZAI_PROXY_URL` | ZAI proxy endpoint for LLM calls (routing and synthesis) | `https://zai-proxy-mcp-apexalgo-iad-ts.ardenone.com:8444/v1/messages` |\n143\t| `ADC_SERVER_URL` | Server URL used by the `adc` CLI | `http://localhost:8000` |\n144\t| `ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL` | Telegram bridge URL for async task notifications (see note below) | `https://telegram-proxy-telegram-bridge-ardenone-cluster-ts.ardenone.com:8444` |\n145\t| `ADC_WHISPER_STT_URL` | Whisper STT service URL for browser speech-to-text fallback | `https://whisper.ardenone.com` |\n146\t\n147\tThe LLM backend for intent routing and synthesis is configured via the `ZAI_PROXY_URL` environment variable. All LLM calls route through the ZAI proxy.\n148\t\n149\t**Telegram bridge (`ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL`) — reachability.** The value above is the Traefik `vpn`-entrypoint route for the `telegram-proxy` service on `ardenone-cluster` (IngressRoute `telegram-proxy-vpn`, namespace `telegram-bridge` → svc `telegram-proxy:8080`). The route and request contract are verified against the live service:\n150\t\n151\t- `GET /health` → `200 {\"ok\":true,\"polling\":true,\"contract_version\":\"1.0\"}`\n152\t- `POST /send` accepts `{chat_id, text, parse_mode}` (returns `405` on GET — endpoint exists, POST-only)\n153\t- `GET /send_message` → `404` (the originally-assumed path does **not** exist)\n154\t\n155\tThe fallback client in `src/telegram/fallback.py` sends to `/send` and probes `/health`, matching this contract. The in-code default (`http://telegram-claude-bridge:8000`) is the in-cluster service name for a future in-cluster deployment; set `ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL` to the `*-ts.ardenone.com` URL above for the local Phase-0 host.\n156\t\n157\t> **DNS caveat (verified 2026-07-19):** on this host the `*.ts.ardenone.com` split-DNS names — this bridge hostname included — do **not** resolve (`getaddrinfo` fails), while other mesh names such as the ZAI proxy do. The route itself is confirmed reachable when the name resolves (e.g. via the cluster's Tailscale egress). Until the DNS record is created, point `ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL` at a resolvable address or add a local/mesh-DNS mapping; otherwise the startup reachability probe fails and `/api/v1/status/telegram_bridge` reports `reachable: false`.\n158\t\n159\t### Prompts directory\n160\t\n161\tAll prompts are hot-reloaded on every invocation. Edit these files to tune behavior without restarting the server:\n162\t\n163\t| File | Purpose |\n164\t|------|---------|\n165\t| `prompts/router.md` | Intent classification and thread segmentation |\n166\t| `prompts/synthesize.md` | Result synthesis (raw data → 2–3 sentence summary) |\n167\t| `prompts/voice.md` | Narration style and pacing |\n168\t| `prompts/urgency.md` | Urgency classification rules |\n169\t\n170\t### Project registry\n171\t\n172\t`config/registry.yaml` defines the projects aide-de-camp knows about. Each entry specifies:\n173\t- `name` and `aliases` — how the router maps utterance fragments to projects\n174\t- `cluster` and `namespace` — where to run kubectl-based fetch commands\n175\t- `workflow_templates` — named multi-step workflows (e.g., \"deploy\", \"rollback\")\n176\t\n177\t---\n178\t\n179\t## Architecture\n180\t\n181\t### Key source files\n182\t\n183\t| File | Responsibility |\n184\t|------|---------------|\n185\t| `src/main.py` | FastAPI app; `/dispatch`, `/voice`, `/sse` endpoints |\n186\t| `src/intent/router.py` | LLM classification and parallel thread dispatch |\n187\t| `src/fetch/commands.py` | Fetch command matrix, intent types, data structures |\n188\t| `src/fetch/orchestrator.py` | Concurrent fetch execution with streaming and coverage tracking (FetchStrand implementation) |\n189\t| `src/synthesize/strand.py` | LLM synthesis strand |\n190\t| `src/session/store.py` | SQLite session store (aiosqlite) |\n191\t| `src/sse/broadcaster.py` | SSE connection registry and event routing |\n192\t| `src/canvas/index.html` | Canvas single-page app |\n193\t| `src/realtime/session.py` | OpenAI Realtime API voice session |\n194\t| `src/watcher/daemon.py` | Async work-item watcher — pushes completed results to canvas |\n195\t| `src/agents/self_modification.py` | Self-improvement agent |\n196\t| `src/environment/discovery.py` | Local and remote repo scanner |\n197\t| `config/registry.yaml` | Explicit project registry |\n198\t| `prompts/` | Hot-reload prompt files |\n199\t\n200\t### Runtime breakdown\n201\t\n202\t| Runtime | Used for |\n203\t|---------|---------|\n204\t| Realtime API | Voice model (persistent session, voice I/O) |\n205\t| Configurable LLM backend | Intent router, synthesize strand |\n206\t| Deterministic code | Fetch strand (kubectl/git execution), bead watcher |\n207\t| Async worker fleet | UI-regen agent, self-modification agent, background analysis, task work items |\n208\t\n209\t### Data flow\n210\t\n211\t```\n212\tPOST /dispatch\n213\t  → intent router (LLM classify)\n214\t  → N parallel: fetch strand → synthesize strand\n215\t  → persist: topic + result in session.db\n216\t  → broadcast SSEEvent(\"result_created\", surface_id)\n217\t  → canvas calls GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics\n218\t  → cards rendered / updated in place\n219\t```\n220\t\n221\t---\n222\t\n223\t## Requirements\n224\t\n225\t- Python 3.11+\n226\t- `fastapi`, `uvicorn[standard]`, `httpx`, `websockets`, `pydantic`, `aiosqlite`\n227\t- Dev: `pytest`, `pytest-asyncio`, `ruff`\n228\t\n229\t---\n230\t\n231\t## Versioning\n232\t\n233\tVersion is in `pyproject.toml` only. No CI build — runs from source.\n234\t\n235\tRelease: bump version in `pyproject.toml` → commit → `git tag vX.Y.Z` → push.\n236\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"395888e3-8489-427d-b265-fb7e23545f8c","uuid":"1fbbac40-f0ec-4644-8aa9-2c45454f0f38","timestamp":"2026-07-23T12:45:28.034Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/README.md","content":"# aide-de-camp\n\n**A personal command interface.** Speak or type a stream-of-consciousness request — spanning multiple projects, questions, and tasks — and receive parallel, organized results on a live browser canvas or in audio.\n\nCLI alias: `adc`\n\n---\n\n## How It Works\n\n1. **Intent routing** — a single fast LLM call segments your utterance into typed intent threads, each tagged with a project, an intent type (`status`, `action`, `brainstorm`, `lookup`, `reminder`, `self-modification`, `monitoring-config`, `task-profile`, `clarification`), and an urgency level (`critical`, `high`, `normal`, `low`).\n\n2. **Parallel dispatch** — all threads run concurrently. A compound utterance touching five projects takes as long as the slowest single agent, not their sum.\n\n3. **Fetch strand** — each agent runs deterministic commands against live data sources (kubectl, git, local repos, SSH targets) to gather raw context for its thread.\n\n4. **Synthesize strand** — one LLM call per thread converts the raw data into a 2–3 sentence structured result.\n\n5. **Canvas delivery** — results stream to the browser via SSE and render as HTML cards, one per topic, updated in place as agents resolve.\n\n```\n  Voice / Text Input\n  \"has the pipeline caught up? also what's the ibkr mcp status?\"\n                        │\n                        ▼\n             ┌──────────────────┐\n             │   Intent Router  │  ← one LLM call, splits utterance\n             └──────────────────┘\n                        │\n          ┌─────────────┴──────────────┐\n          ▼                            ▼\n   [agent: pipeline]           [agent: ibkr-mcp]\n   Fetch → Synthesize           Fetch → Synthesize\n          │                            │\n          └──────────────┬─────────────┘\n                         ▼\n              ┌─────────────────────────┐\n              │  Canvas (SSE)           │\n              │  ┌─────────┐ ┌───────┐  │\n              │  │pipeline │ │ibkr   │  │\n              │  │ card    │ │ card  │  │\n              │  └─────────┘ └───────┘  │\n              └─────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n**Task-profile intents** skip fetch/synthesize. Instead, a work item is created and picked up asynchronously by an async worker fleet. When the work completes, the result is pushed to the canvas via the bead watcher daemon.\n\n---\n\n## Features\n\n### Input methods\n- **Text**: browser canvas at `http://localhost:8000/` or CLI: `adc dispatch \"...\"`\n- **Voice**: OpenAI Realtime API (WebRTC) — speak directly into the browser\n\n### Live canvas\nSingle-page dark-themed UI (`src/canvas/index.html`). Results stream in via Server-Sent Events. Each distinct topic (project + intent) gets its own card, updated in place as results arrive. No page reload needed.\n\n### Voice narration\nResults are narrated at urgency-tiered conversational pauses:\n- `critical` — interrupt immediately\n- `high` — next natural pause\n- `normal` — batched delivery\n- `low` — only when idle\n\n### Self-modification and hot-reload\nAll behavior-defining artifacts are plain files, re-read on every invocation:\n- `prompts/router.md`, `prompts/synthesize.md`, `prompts/voice.md`, `prompts/urgency.md`\n- `config/registry.yaml` — project and alias definitions\n- `config/monitoring.yaml` — ambient monitoring rules\n- `data/components.db` — versioned HTML/CSS card templates (SQLite)\n\nA spoken instruction like \"the deploy card is too cluttered\" triggers the `SelfModificationAgent`, which generates a diff, surfaces it for approval, and hot-reloads the artifact. No redeploy needed.\n\n### Project registry\n`config/registry.yaml` defines explicit project entries with cluster targets, namespaces, aliases, and workflow templates. aide-de-camp also auto-discovers git repos at startup. Refresh via `POST /api/v1/environment/refresh`.\n\n### Session persistence\nSession state lives in a SQLite WAL database (`data/session.db`). Tables: `sessions`, `surfaces`, `utterances`, `intents`, `topics`, `results`. The same session is accessible from the canvas, CLI, or audio mode simultaneously.\n\n---\n\n## Quick Start\n\n```bash\n# Clone and install\ngit clone <repo-url> aide-de-camp\ncd aide-de-camp\n\npip install -e \".[dev]\"\n# or\npip install -r requirements.txt\n\n# Start the server\nuvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000\n\n# Verify it's running\ncurl -s http://localhost:8000/health\n```\n\nOpen `http://localhost:8000/` in your browser to use the canvas.\n\nFor voice mode, an OpenAI API key with Realtime API access is required (set `OPENAI_API_KEY`).\n\n---\n\n## CLI Usage\n\n```bash\n# Route an utterance and stream results to the canvas\nadc dispatch \"has the options pipeline caught up?\"\n\n# Query a specific topic\nadc ask \"what name candidates did we have for pdftract?\"\n\n# Current session summary\nadc status\n\n# List active topic cards\nadc topics\n\n# Show exception queue\nadc exceptions\n\n# Configure the CLI\nadc config --set-server http://myhost:8000\nadc config --set-session <session-id>\n\n# Help\nadc --help\n```\n\n---\n\n## Configuration\n\n### Environment variables\n\n| Variable | Purpose | Default |\n|----------|---------|---------|\n| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | OpenAI key — required for voice/Realtime API | _(none; voice disabled without it)_ |\n| `ZAI_PROXY_URL` | ZAI proxy endpoint for LLM calls (routing and synthesis) | `https://zai-proxy-mcp-apexalgo-iad-ts.ardenone.com:8444/v1/messages` |\n| `ADC_SERVER_URL` | Server URL used by the `adc` CLI | `http://localhost:8000` |\n| `ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL` | Telegram bridge URL for async task notifications (see note below) | `https://telegram-proxy-telegram-bridge-ardenone-cluster-ts.ardenone.com:8444` |\n| `ADC_WHISPER_STT_URL` | Whisper STT service URL for browser speech-to-text fallback | `https://whisper.ardenone.com` |\n\nThe LLM backend for intent routing and synthesis is configured via the `ZAI_PROXY_URL` environment variable. All LLM calls route through the ZAI proxy.\n\n**Telegram bridge (`ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL`) — reachability.** The value above is the Traefik `vpn`-entrypoint route for the `telegram-proxy` service on `ardenone-cluster` (IngressRoute `telegram-proxy-vpn`, namespace `telegram-bridge` → svc `telegram-proxy:8080`). The route and request contract are verified against the live service:\n\n- `GET /health` → `200 {\"ok\":true,\"polling\":true,\"contract_version\":\"1.0\"}`\n- `POST /send` accepts `{chat_id, text, parse_mode}` (returns `405` on GET — endpoint exists, POST-only)\n- `GET /send_message` → `404` (the originally-assumed path does **not** exist)\n\nThe fallback client in `src/telegram/fallback.py` sends to `/send` and probes `/health`, matching this contract. The in-code default (`http://telegram-claude-bridge:8000`) is the in-cluster service name for a future in-cluster deployment; set `ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL` to the `*-ts.ardenone.com` URL above for the local Phase-0 host.\n\n> **DNS caveat (verified 2026-07-19):** on this host the `*.ts.ardenone.com` split-DNS names — this bridge hostname included — do **not** resolve (`getaddrinfo` fails), while other mesh names such as the ZAI proxy do. The route itself is confirmed reachable when the name resolves (e.g. via the cluster's Tailscale egress). Edit these files to tune behavior without restarting the server:\n\n| File | Purpose |\n|------|---------|\n| `prompts/router.md` | Intent classification and thread segmentation |\n| `prompts/synthesize.md` | Result synthesis (raw data → 2–3 sentence summary) |\n| `prompts/voice.md` | Narration style and pacing |\n| `prompts/urgency.md` | Urgency classification rules |\n\n### Project registry\n\n`config/registry.yaml` defines the projects aide-de-camp knows about. Each entry specifies:\n- `name` and `aliases` — how the router maps utterance fragments to projects\n- `cluster` and `namespace` — where to run kubectl-based fetch commands\n- `workflow_templates` — named multi-step workflows (e.g., \"deploy\", \"rollback\")\n\n---\n\n## Architecture\n\n### Key source files\n\n| File | Responsibility |\n|------|---------------|\n| `src/main.py` | FastAPI app; `/dispatch`, `/voice`, `/sse` endpoints |\n| `src/intent/router.py` | LLM classification and parallel thread dispatch |\n| `src/fetch/commands.py` | Fetch command matrix, intent types, data structures |\n| `src/fetch/orchestrator.py` | Concurrent fetch execution with streaming and coverage tracking (FetchStrand implementation) |\n| `src/synthesize/strand.py` | LLM synthesis strand |\n| `src/session/store.py` | SQLite session store (aiosqlite) |\n| `src/sse/broadcaster.py` | SSE connection registry and event routing |\n| `src/canvas/index.html` | Canvas single-page app |\n| `src/realtime/session.py` | OpenAI Realtime API voice session |\n| `src/watcher/daemon.py` | Async work-item watcher — pushes completed results to canvas |\n| `src/agents/self_modification.py` | Self-improvement agent |\n| `src/environment/discovery.py` | Local and remote repo scanner |\n| `config/registry.yaml` | Explicit project registry |\n| `prompts/` | Hot-reload prompt files |\n\n### Runtime breakdown\n\n| Runtime | Used for |\n|---------|---------|\n| Realtime API | Voice model (persistent session, voice I/O) |\n| Configurable LLM backend | Intent router, synthesize strand |\n| Deterministic code | Fetch strand (kubectl/git execution), bead watcher |\n| Async worker fleet | UI-regen agent, self-modification agent, background analysis, task work items |\n\n### Data flow\n\n```\nPOST /dispatch\n  → intent router (LLM classify)\n  → N parallel: fetch strand → synthesize strand\n  → persist: topic + result in session.db\n  → broadcast SSEEvent(\"result_created\", surface_id)\n  → canvas calls GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics\n  → cards rendered / updated in place\n```\n\n---\n\n## Requirements\n\n- Python 3.11+\n- `fastapi`, `uvicorn[standard]`, `httpx`, `websockets`, `pydantic`, `aiosqlite`\n- Dev: `pytest`, `pytest-asyncio`, `ruff`\n\n---\n\n## Versioning\n\nVersion is in `pyproject.toml` only. \"synthesize_total_ms\",\n232\t    \"escalate_ms\",\n233\t    \"sse_emit_ms\",\n234\t    \"stt_ms\",\n235\t    \"first_render_ms\",\n236\t)\n237\t\n238\t# Valid intent status values (plan §10 The Async Path: stuck/failed added for circuit breaker)\n239\tINTENT_STATUSES = ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled', 'stuck', 'failed')\n240\t\n241\t# Default SLA hours per intent type (plan §10 The Async Path: Visible Aging)\n242\t# task-profile: 6h (async bead-backed tasks)\n243\t# hot-path intents: 30s (budget is 3s, flag at 10x for aged pending)\n244\tDEFAULT_SLA_HOURS: dict[str, float] = {\n245\t    \"task-profile\": 6.0,  # 6 hours for async bead-backed tasks\n246\t    \"status\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path status intents\n247\t    \"action\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path action intents\n248\t    \"lookup\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path lookup intents\n249\t    \"brainstorm\": 0.5,   # 30 minutes for brainstorm (may need user iteration)\n250\t    \"reminder\": 24.0,    # 24 hours for reminders\n251\t}\n252\t\n253\t# Circuit breaker thresholds (plan §10 The Async Path)\n254\tCIRCUIT_BREAKER_REFUSAL_THRESHOLD = 3  # Fence after N refusals\n255\tCIRCUIT_BREAKER_AGE_THRESHOLD_HOURS = 24.0  # Fence after N hours without progress\n256\t\n257\t\n258\tclass SessionStore:\n259\t    \"\"\"Session store with WAL mode for concurrent access.\"\"\"\n260\t\n261\t    def __init__(self, db_path: Path):\n262\t        self.db_path = db_path\n263\t\n264\t    async def initialize(self) -> None:\n265\t        \"\"\"Initialize database with schema and WAL mode.\"\"\"\n266\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n267\t            # Enable WAL mode for concurrent reads during writes\n268\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\n269\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n270\t\n271\t            # Create schema\n272\t            await db.executescript(SCHEMA_SQL)\n273\t            await db.commit()\n274\t\n275\t            # Additive migrations: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS above creates new\n276\t            # columns only for freshly-made DBs; an existing data/session.db\n277\t            # (e.g. the live Hetzner box) keeps its old column set and must be\n278\t            # ALTERed. Each statement is guarded by a PRAGMA table_info probe so\n279\t            # it is idempotent and never re-runs once the column exists.\n280\t            await self._migrate_additive_columns(db)\n281\t\n282\t    @staticmethod\n283\t    async def _migrate_additive_columns(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n284\t        \"\"\"Idempotently add columns introduced after the initial schema.\"\"\"\n285\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(results)\") as cur:\n286\t            result_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n287\t        if \"result_type\" not in result_cols:\n288\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN result_type TEXT\")\n289\t        if \"card_fallback\" not in result_cols:\n290\t            await db.execute(\n291\t                \"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0\"\n292\t            )\n293\t\n294\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n295\t            intent_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n296\t        if \"lookup_kind\" not in intent_cols:\n297\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents ADD COLUMN lookup_kind TEXT\")\n298\t\n299\t        # Migrate intents.status CHECK constraint to include 'stuck' and 'failed'\n300\t        # SQLite doesn't support ALTER CONSTRAINT, so we need to recreate the table\n301\t        # First, check if the table still has the old constraint (without stuck/failed)\n302\t        await SessionStore._migrate_intents_status_enum(db)\n303\t\n304\t        # Migrate results.intent_id to allow NULL for monitoring-originated results\n305\t        await SessionStore._migrate_results_intent_id_nullable(db)\n306\t\n307\t        # Migrate bead_watch.comment_high_water default from 0 to -1\n308\t        await SessionStore._migrate_bead_watch_comment_high_water(db)\n309\t\n310\t        await db.commit()\n311\t\n312\t    @staticmethod\n313\t    async def _migrate_intents_status_enum(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n314\t        \"\"\"Migrate intents table to support 'stuck' and 'failed' status values.\n315\t\n316\t        Plan §10 The Async Path: extend intents status enum with stuck/failed.\n317\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER CONSTRAINT, so we recreate the table.\n318\t        \"\"\"\n319\t        # Check if migration is needed by looking for 'stuck' in the schema\n320\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n321\t            cols = await cur.fetchall()\n322\t            status_col = None\n323\t            for col in cols:\n324\t                if col[1] == \"status\":\n325\t                    status_col = col\n326\t                    break\n327\t\n328\t        # If no status column or already has stuck/failed, skip migration\n329\t        if not status_col:\n330\t            return\n331\t\n332\t        # Check if the CHECK constraint already includes stuck/failed\n333\t        # by inspecting the SQL for the intents table\n334\t        async with db.execute(\n335\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='intents'\"\n336\t        ) as cur:\n337\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n338\t            if table_sql and (\"'stuck'\" in table_sql[0] or \"'failed'\" in table_sql[0]):\n339\t                # Already migrated\n340\t                return\n341\t\n342\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with new constraint\n343\t        logger.info(\"Migrating intents.status to include 'stuck' and 'failed' values\")\n344\t\n345\t        # Begin transaction for data migration\n346\t        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n347\t\n348\t        try:\n349\t            # Create new table with updated constraint\n350\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n351\t                CREATE TABLE intents_new (\n352\t                    id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n353\t                    utterance_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n354\t                    session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n355\t                    topic_id     TEXT,\n356\t                    project_slug TEXT,\n357\t                    intent_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n358\t                    lookup_kind  TEXT,\n359\t                    status       TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled', 'stuck', 'failed')) DEFAULT 'pending',\n360\t                    bead_ref     TEXT,\n361\t                    created_at   INTEGER NOT NULL,\n362\t                    resolved_at  INTEGER,\n363\t                    FOREIGN KEY (utterance_id) REFERENCES utterances(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n364\t                    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n365\t                    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n366\t                )\n367\t            \"\"\")\n368\t\n369\t            # Copy data from old table to new table\n370\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n371\t                INSERT INTO intents_new\n372\t                SELECT id, utterance_id, session_id, topic_id, project_slug,\n373\t                       intent_type, lookup_kind, status, bead_ref, created_at, resolved_at\n374\t                FROM intents\n375\t            \"\"\")\n376\t\n377\t            # With DEFAULT -1, no comments are skipped\n490\t        initially, and only comments with index > comment_high_water are processed.\n491\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER COLUMN DEFAULT, so we recreate the table.\n492\t        \"\"\"\n493\t        # Check if migration is needed by inspecting the bead_watch table schema\n494\t        async with db.execute(\n495\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='bead_watch'\"\n496\t        ) as cur:\n497\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n498\t            if not table_sql:\n499\t                return  # Table doesn't exist yet\n500\t\n501\t            # If comment_high_water already has DEFAULT -1, skip migration\n502\t            if \"DEFAULT -1\" in table_sql[0]:\n503\t                # Already migrated\n504\t                return\n505\t\n506\t        # Copy data from old table to new table\n529\t            # Existing rows with comment_high_water=0 are migrated as-is\n530\t            # (they represent \"comment 0 has been processed\", which is valid)\n531\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n532\t                INSERT INTO bead_watch_new\n533\t                SELECT * FROM bead_watch\n534\t            \"\"\")\n535\t\n536\t            # aiosqlite uses connection-per-context, so just ensure checkpoint\n557\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n558\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)\")\n559\t\n560\t    #
